---
title: "Introduction to B2CViz"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Introduction to B2CViz}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

# Introduction

B2CViz is a package designed to help visualize and analyze VisiumHD spatial single-cell data that has been segmented with Bin2cell or Spaceranger. This vignette covers:

1. Loading data and building a B2C object
2. Selecting regions of interest and visualizing features
3. Computing pairwise cell-cell distances
4. Running the neighborhood analysis pipeline
5. Converting B2C objects for hoodscanR / Statial analysis

# Setup

B2CViz and its required packages are loaded automatically when you call `library(B2CViz)`. The following optional Bioconductor packages are needed for the hoodscanR integration and heatmap plotting functions:

``` r
BiocManager::install(c("SpatialExperiment", "hoodscanR", "SingleCellExperiment",
                       "circlize", "ComplexHeatmap"))
```

# Loading Data

## Bin2cell workflow

Two Seurat objects are needed: the pre-aggregated object (after `b2c.expand_labels`) and the post-aggregated object (after `b2c.bin_to_cell`). Convert them from AnnData with [sceasy](https://github.com/cellgeni/sceasy) and load them as follows:

``` r
library(B2CViz)

obj_pre  <- readRDS("/path/to/pre_object.rds")
obj_post <- readRDS("/path/to/post_object.rds")
image_path <- "/path/to/image.jpg"

obj_post <- NormalizeData(obj_post)

b2c <- load_b2c(pre = obj_pre, post = obj_post, path = image_path)
```

## Spaceranger workflow

``` r
data.dir <- "/path/to/outs/"
slice    <- "slice1"

obj <- Load10X_Spatial(data.dir = data.dir, slice = slice, bin.size = "polygons")
sf  <- obj@images[[paste0(slice, ".polygons")]]@scale.factors$hires

image_path <- "/path/to/outs/segmented_outputs/spatial/tissue_hires_image.png"
b2c <- load_b2c(post = obj, path = image_path,
                data = "spaceranger", slice = slice, scale.factor = sf)

b2c$post <- NormalizeData(b2c$post)
```

# Selecting Regions of Interest

Use `set_roi()` to open an interactive Shiny app where you can draw rectangle or polygon ROIs. Then crop the B2C object to each ROI.

``` r
b2c   <- set_roi(b2c = b2c)
b2c_1 <- crop_b2c(b2c, roi = 1)
b2c_2 <- crop_b2c(b2c, roi = 2)
```

Optionally, replace the downscaled tissue image with a region extracted from the original OME-TIFF for best quality:

``` r
b2c_1 <- upscale_roi(b2c_1, "/path/to/original_OME.tiff")
```

# Visualizing Features

## Whole-slide overview

``` r
b2c <- scaledown_img(b2c = b2c)
overview_b2c(b2c = b2c, feat = "CDH1")
```

## Segmentation inspection

``` r
plot_segmentation(b2c = b2c_1)
```

## Feature plots with `plot_b2c`

`plot_b2c` accepts a wide range of parameters to control appearance. A
few examples:

``` r
# Basic plot
plot_b2c(b2c = b2c_1, feat = "CDH1")

# Adjust transparency, threshold, and H&E visibility
plot_b2c(b2c = b2c_1,
         feat      = "CDH1",
         alpha.low  = 0.1,
         min.visible = 2,
         he_alpha   = 0.1,
         col.high   = "orangered")

# Multiple features with per-feature parameter vectors
plot_b2c(b2c = b2c_1,
         feat       = c("CDH1", "CD4"),
         min.visible = c(2, 2.5),
         col.high   = c("orangered", "seagreen2"))

# Discrete metadata column (e.g. cluster labels)
plot_b2c(b2c = b2c_1, feat = "seurat_clusters",
         plot.type = "hulls", discrete.alpha = 0.8)

# Highlight specific cells
plot_b2c(b2c = b2c_1, feat = "CDH1",
         outline.hulls = c(154900, 157962, 157507))

# Pre-filter to cells above a threshold in another feature
plot_b2c(b2c = b2c_1, feat = "CDH1",
         filter.feat = "CLU", filter.threshold = 5)
```

# Quantification

## Cell-cell distances with `get_dist`

Call `plot_b2c` with `plot = FALSE` to obtain the internal data structure,
then pass it to `get_dist`. `get_dist` returns a **list**:

- `$distances` — data frame of all origin–neighbor pairs within `radius`, with
  columns `origin_name`, `origin_marker`, `origin_value`, `neighbor_name`,
  `neighbor_marker`, `neighbor_value`, `distance`
- `$locations` — data frame of all displayed cells with columns `cell_id`,
  `x`, `y`

The `plot_b2c` return value also includes `$step`, the median nearest-neighbour
step size in plot coordinates (needed to scale distances to microns downstream).

``` r
p <- plot_b2c(b2c = b2c_1, feat = c("CDH1", "seurat_clusters"), plot = FALSE)
d <- get_dist(p, radius = 500)

# quick distance distribution
plot_dist(d$distances, binwidth = 20)
```

## Neighborhood analysis pipeline

The pipeline takes a combined, annotated edge table from one or more ROIs and
conditions, runs a series of summaries, and supports group comparisons.

### 1. Collect and annotate get_dist output

``` r
p1 <- plot_b2c(b2c = b2c_1, feat = c("CDH1", "seurat_clusters"), plot = FALSE)
p2 <- plot_b2c(b2c = b2c_2, feat = c("CDH1", "seurat_clusters"), plot = FALSE)

d1 <- get_dist(p1, radius = 500)
d2 <- get_dist(p2, radius = 500)

d1$distances$roi   <- "roi_1"
d1$distances$group <- "tumor"
d2$distances$roi   <- "roi_2"
d2$distances$group <- "stroma"

combined <- rbind(d1$distances, d2$distances)
```

### 2. Standardize column names

`standardize_get_dist` renames and type-coerces columns to the canonical
format required by all downstream functions:

``` r
edges <- standardize_get_dist(combined,
                              origin_type_col   = "origin_value",
                              neighbor_type_col = "neighbor_value")
```

### 3. Optional QC

``` r
qc <- check_get_dist_table(edges)
str(qc)
```

### 4. Run the full pipeline

`mean_step` (from `p$step`) rescales the distance breakpoints from internal
plot units to microns:

``` r
res <- run_full_pipeline(edges, mean_step = p1$step)
```

The result is a named list:

| Element | Description |
|---|---|
| `$qc` | Sanity checks on the edge table |
| `$pair_stats` | Per-ROI pairwise neighborhood statistics |
| `$radial_stats` | Per-ROI radial distance-bin profiles |
| `$cumulative_stats` | Cumulative neighbor counts at a series of radii |
| `$group_pair_stats` | Cross-ROI group-level summaries |
| `$cells` | Cell-type abundance per ROI |

Optionally rescale distance columns back to microns:

``` r
res <- rescale_res_distances(res, factor = p1$step)
```

### 5. Compare groups

``` r
cmp <- compare_groups(res$pair_stats,
                      metric  = "mean_neighbors_per_origin",
                      group_a = "tumor",
                      group_b = "stroma")
head(cmp)
```

### 6. Plot results

``` r
# Point plot: mean neighbors per origin, faceted by neighbor type
plot_stats(res, table = "pair_stats", focus = "Tumor",
           x = "group", y = "mean_neighbors_per_origin",
           facet_y = "neighbor_type", group = "group")

# Radial profile across distance bins
plot_stats(res, table = "radial_stats", focus = "Tumor",
           x = "dist_bin", y = "mean_neighbors_per_origin_bin",
           facet_y = "neighbor_type", group = "group")

# Group-level heatmap (requires ComplexHeatmap and circlize)
plot_hm(res, focus = "Tumor", metric = "mean_neighbors_per_origin_mean")

# 2-D neighbor density map centered on a focal origin cell type
plot_density(list(roi_1 = d1), roi = "roi_1",
             origin = "Tumor", neighbor = "T cell", n = 50)
```

### 7. Handling ROI border effects

Origin cells near the ROI boundary have incomplete neighborhood spheres.
`trim_origins_by_border` removes them before summarizing. This can also be
triggered via `border_trim = TRUE` in `run_full_pipeline`:

``` r
roi_windows <- data.frame(
  roi  = "roi_1",
  xmin = 0, xmax = 2000,
  ymin = 0, ymax = 2000
)

res <- run_full_pipeline(edges, mean_step = p1$step,
                         border_trim  = TRUE,
                         coords       = d1$locations,
                         roi_windows  = roi_windows)
```

# hoodscanR / Statial Integration

B2CViz objects can be converted to a `SpatialExperiment` for downstream
analysis with [hoodscanR](https://bioconductor.org/packages/hoodscanR) or
[Statial](https://bioconductor.org/packages/Statial).

## Convert to SpatialExperiment

``` r
# annot_col is the metadata column containing cell type labels
spe <- b2c_to_hood(b2c_1$post, annot_col = "seurat_clusters")
```

## Compute a co-localization correlation matrix

`get_cor_hood` runs the complete hoodscanR pipeline (neighbor detection,
scanning, merging, and metric calculation) and returns a cell-type
co-localization correlation matrix:

``` r
cor_mat <- get_cor_hood(spe, k = 100)
```

## Extract the upper triangle

`get_upper` converts the symmetric matrix to a tidy data frame of unique
cell-type pairs:

``` r
upper_df <- get_upper(cor_mat)
head(upper_df)
#   celltype1 celltype2         r
# 1    B cell    T cell  0.312
# ...
```

## Focal neighborhood plot

`plot_hood_focal` filters and harmonizes an edge table so that a chosen focal
cell type always appears as `celltype1`, then plots a faceted summary:

``` r
plot_hood_focal(edge_df = upper_df,
                focus   = "Tumor",
                x       = "sample",
                metric  = "r",
                facet_y = "celltype2",
                group   = "condition")
```

## Convert edge table back to a matrix

Useful for custom heatmaps or export:

``` r
mat <- edge_table_to_matrix(upper_df,
                            value_col     = "r",
                            celltype1_col = "celltype1",
                            celltype2_col = "celltype2")
```
